tool_name: Steel Welded Connection Design Calculator - EN 1993-1-8 AWS D1.1
category: Structural Engineering / Steel Connections / Welded Connection Design
calculation_basis: Steel welded connection design screening using EN 1993-1-8 and AWS D1.1/AISC-style design checks for fillet welds, complete-joint-penetration groove welds, partial-joint-penetration groove welds, weld throat area, effective weld length, weld group centroid, eccentric weld group demand, direct shear, normal tension, torsional/moment demand, weld metal resistance, base metal yielding, base metal rupture, HAZ/local plate checks, lamellar tearing review, fatigue review, seismic review, WPS/PQR/welder qualification, NDT/inspection readiness, distortion/heat-input review and governing demand-capacity ratio. The output is a preliminary engineering screening calculation and must not replace sealed structural design, project national annex, AWS D1.1 detailed design, welding procedure specification qualification, inspection plan, fatigue design, seismic design, fire design, erection method statement or authority approval.

inputs.label: Design Code Selection
inputs.description: Select governing design code for weld resistance calculations

inputs.label: EN National Annex (if EN selected)
inputs.description: Select applicable National Annex for EN 1993-1-8 partial factors

inputs.label: Weld Type
inputs.description: Select the weld configuration type

inputs.label: Connection Type
inputs.description: Select the structural connection configuration
options: Base Plate to Column — Fillet or Partial Penetration

inputs.label: Base Material Grade — Connected Part
inputs.description: Steel grade of the primary connected member
options: ASTM A992 (345 MPa yield, Fy=50 ksi)

inputs.label: Connected Part Thickness (t_conn)
inputs.description: Thickness of the base material being welded (the thinner part for dissimilar thickness)

inputs.label: Base Material Grade — Other Part (if different)
inputs.description: Steel grade of the other connected member (if different from first)

inputs.label: Other Part Thickness (t_other)
inputs.description: Thickness of the other base material being welded

inputs.label: Electrode / Filler Metal Classification
inputs.description: Select the filler metal classification per EN ISO or AWS A5 standards
options: EN ISO 2560-A: E 38 X (Fu=380–440 MPa, matched to S235)
EN ISO 2560-A: E 42 X (Fu=420–500 MPa, matched to S275)
EN ISO 2560-A: E 46 X (Fu=460–560 MPa, matched to S355)
EN ISO 2560-A: E 50 X (Fu=500–600 MPa, overmatch)
AWS A5.1: E70XX (Fu=480 MPa, most common)

inputs.label: Electrode Nominal Tensile Strength (Fu_E)
inputs.description: Nominal tensile strength of the filler metal from the selected classification

inputs.label: Welding Process
inputs.description: Select the welding process for WPS qualification reference

inputs.label: Welding Position
inputs.description: Welding position per EN ISO 6947 / AWS D1.1. Affects effective throat and qualified prequalification.

inputs.label: Fillet Weld Leg Size (s)
inputs.description: Nominal fillet weld leg length (for fillet weld design)

inputs.label: Fillet Weld Effective Throat Thickness (a)
inputs.description: Effective throat thickness. For equal-leg fillet: a = s / sqrt(2). For unequal-leg or deep-penetration, use calculated or measured value.

inputs.label: Groove Weld Effective Depth (d_groove)
inputs.description: Effective depth of groove weld (for PJP: specified depth of bevel; for CJP: full thickness)

inputs.label: Effective Weld Length (L_w)
inputs.description: Effective length of the weld segment. For end returns, include return length per code provisions.

inputs.label: Number of Weld Segments (n_seg)
inputs.description: Number of weld lines / segments in the weld group

inputs.label: Weld Group Eccentricity — X Direction (e_x)
inputs.description: Eccentricity of applied load from weld group centroid in the x-direction (creates torsional moment)

inputs.label: Weld Group Eccentricity — Y Direction (e_y)
inputs.description: Eccentricity of applied load from weld group centroid in the y-direction

inputs.label: Weld Group Eccentricity — Out-of-Plane (e_z)
inputs.description: Out-of-plane eccentricity (creates bending moment in the weld group)

inputs.label: Applied Axial Tension (N_Ed)
inputs.description: Factored axial tension load applied to the connection

inputs.label: Applied Shear Force (V_Ed)
inputs.description: Factored shear force applied to the connection

inputs.label: Applied Bending Moment (M_Ed)
inputs.description: Factored bending moment applied to the connection (includes moment from eccentricity if applicable)

inputs.label: Applied Torsional Moment (T_Ed)
inputs.description: Factored torsional moment applied to the weld group

inputs.label: Load Type — Cyclic / Fatigue
inputs.description: Indicates whether the connection is subject to significant cyclic loading (fatigue assessment required per EN 1993-1-9 or AWS D1.1 Clause 2)

inputs.label: Fatigue Stress Range (Delta_sigma)
inputs.description: Nominal stress range at the weld detail for fatigue assessment. Only required if Load Type is cyclic.

inputs.label: Fatigue Detail Category (FAT Class)
inputs.description: Detail category per EN 1993-1-9 Table 8.1–8.10 or AWS D1.1 fatigue curves
options: FAT 160 — Butt weld, ground flush, 100% NDT
FAT 140 — Butt weld, ground, no misalignment
FAT 112 — Butt weld as-welded, normal quality
FAT 45 — unstressed weld in tension zone
FAT 36 — base metal at weld toe, as-welded

inputs.label: Number of Fatigue Cycles (N_f)
inputs.description: Expected number of constant-amplitude stress cycles during design life

inputs.label: Seismic Application (Ductility Demand)
inputs.description: Seismic design category and ductility demand on weld. Affects required toughness and detailing per EN 1998-1 / AWS D1.1 Cl. 7 (AWS D1.8) or AISC 341.
options: Seismic force-resisting system (SFRS) — weld demand critical

inputs.label: Joint Type / Configuration Detail
inputs.description: Detailed joint configuration per EN ISO 9692-1 or AWS D1.1 prequalified joint details
options: Lap joint — two fillet welds (load transfer)
Butt joint — square edge (t <= 5mm, no bevel)
Weld access hole — beam flange groove (RBS type)

inputs.label: Surface Preparation Condition
inputs.description: Surface preparation condition of base material before welding. Affects quality and strength assumptions.
options: Clean mill scale (as-rolled, acceptable for most cases)
Grinding of weld area (removing mill scale)

inputs.label: Preheat Temperature (T_preheat)
inputs.description: Minimum preheat temperature per EN 1011-2 / AWS D1.1 Table 3.3 based on thickness, CEV, and hydrogen level

inputs.label: Interpass Temperature (T_interpass)
inputs.description: Maximum interpass temperature per WPS. Typically 200–300°C for structural steels

inputs.label: Heat Input (HI)
inputs.description: Welding heat input per unit length. Calculated from arc energy: HI = (V × I × efficiency) / travel_speed. Affects HAZ properties and cooling rate.

inputs.label: Carbon Equivalent (CEV / CET)
inputs.description: Carbon equivalent value per EN 1011-2 (IIW formula or CET). Used to determine preheat requirements and hardenability risk.

inputs.label: Hydrogen Level Class (per ISO 3690)
inputs.description: Diffusible hydrogen level of the filler metal per ISO 3690. Affects preheat and hydrogen cracking risk.
options: Class D (high, > 15 mL/100g — avoid for structural steel)

inputs.label: Weld Quality Level (EN ISO 5817 / AWS D1.1)
inputs.description: Acceptance quality level for weld imperfections. Higher level means stricter acceptance criteria.
options: EN ISO 5817:2014 — Level C (intermediate, general structural)

inputs.label: NDT / Inspection Level Required
inputs.description: Level of non-destructive testing required for the weld based on EN 1090-2 execution class or AWS D1.1 inspection requirements
options: EXC4 — VT + 100% RT/UT + MT/PT (full NDT regime)
AWS D1.1 — VT + UT per Clause 8 (static)
AWS D1.1 — VT + UT + MT per Clause 9 (cyclic)
AWS D1.1 — VT + RT per Clause 10 (tubular)

inputs.label: EN 1090-2 Execution Class
inputs.description: Execution class per EN 1090-2 based on consequence class, service category, and production category. Determines fabrication and inspection requirements.

inputs.label: Corrosion Protection Requirement
inputs.description: Corrosion protection system per ISO 12944. Determines coating system and surface preparation for weld zones.
options: C1 — Heated interior (no coating, minimal protection)

inputs.label: Minimum Service Temperature (T_min)
inputs.description: Minimum anticipated service temperature. Affects Charpy toughness requirements and risk of brittle fracture per EN 1993-1-10 / AWS D1.1 Clause 3.

inputs.label: Governing Thickness for Toughness (t_eff)
inputs.description: Governing thickness for fracture toughness assessment per EN 1993-1-10. Typically the thickest tension element in the connection.

inputs.label: Required Charpy V-Notch Energy (CVN) at T_min
inputs.description: Required minimum Charpy V-notch impact energy at the minimum service temperature. For EN 1993-1-10 or AWS D1.1 Clause 3 requirements.

inputs.label: Stress Concentration Factor (K_t)
inputs.description: Stress concentration factor at the weld toe/root detail. Use 1.0 for smooth transition, or higher values for severe notch effects.

inputs.label: Partial Factor — Weld Resistance (gamma_M2)
inputs.description: EN 1993-1-8 §2.2(2): partial factor for weld resistance. Recommended 1.25 for resistance of welds. Check National Annex for project-specific value.

inputs.label: Partial Factor — Cross-Section Resistance (gamma_M0)
inputs.description: EN 1993-1-1 §6.1: partial factor for cross-section resistance. Recommended 1.00. Check National Annex.

inputs.label: Partial Factor — Fatigue (gamma_Mf)
inputs.description: EN 1993-1-9 §3.7: partial factor for fatigue strength. Recommended 1.00 for low damage tolerance, 1.15 for high. Check National Annex.

inputs.label: Inherent Welding Strain (Low Estimate)
inputs.description: Assumed inherent longitudinal welding strain for distortion estimation (based on heat input and restraint)

inputs.label: Electrode Ultimate Tensile Strength (f_u,elec)
inputs.description: Nominal ultimate tensile strength of weld electrode material per EN ISO 2560 / AWS A5.1

inputs.label: Factored Axial Force (N_Ed)
inputs.description: Factored axial force per EN 1990 / EN 1993-1-1

inputs.label: Factored Shear Force (V_Ed)
inputs.description: Factored shear force per EN 1990 / EN 1993-1-1

inputs.label: Factored Torsion (T_Ed)
inputs.description: Factored torsional moment per EN 1993-1-8

inputs.label: Factored Moment (M_Ed)
inputs.description: Factored bending moment per EN 1993-1-8

formulas.label: Fillet Weld Effective Throat Thickness
formulas.description: Effective throat thickness for equal-leg fillet weld per EN 1993-1-8 §4.3.2.1(1). For unequal legs, use shorter leg × cos(angle).
formulas.expression: a_eff = (weld_type == "Fillet Weld (double-sided)" || weld_type == "Fillet Weld (single-sided)") ? fillet_weld_leg_size_mm / Sqrt(2) : fillet_weld_throat_thickness_mm

formulas.label: Total Weld Effective Area
formulas.description: Total effective throat area of the weld group per EN 1993-1-8 §4.3.2.1(2). For fillet welds: A_w = sum(a_eff_i × L_eff_i).

formulas.label: Base Material Ultimate Tensile Strength
formulas.description: Ultimate tensile strength of the base material based on selected steel grade per EN 10025 or ASTM
formulas.expression: fu_bm = (base_material_grade_connected == 0 ?   360 : base_material_grade_connected == 1 ? 360 : base_material_grade_connected == 2 ? 430 : base_material_grade_connected == 3 ? 470 : base_material_grade_connected == 4 ? 510 : base_material_grade_connected == 5 ? 540 : base_material_grade_connected == 6 ? 650 : base_material_grade_connected == 7 ? 400 : base_material_grade_connected == 8 ? 450 : base_material_grade_connected == 9 ? 450 : base_material_grade_connected == 10 ? 400 : base_material_grade_connected == 11 ? 760 : 450)

formulas.label: Base Material Yield Strength
formulas.description: Nominal yield strength of the base material based on selected grade
formulas.expression: fy_bm = (base_material_grade_connected == 0 ?   235 : base_material_grade_connected == 1 ? 235 : base_material_grade_connected == 2 ? 275 : base_material_grade_connected == 3 ? 355 : base_material_grade_connected == 4 ? 420 : base_material_grade_connected == 5 ? 460 : base_material_grade_connected == 6 ? 600 : base_material_grade_connected == 7 ? 250 : base_material_grade_connected == 8 ? 345 : base_material_grade_connected == 9 ? 345 : base_material_grade_connected == 10 ? 315 : base_material_grade_connected == 11 ? 690 : 345)

formulas.label: Correlation Factor for Fillet Weld (beta_w)
formulas.description: Correlation factor per EN 1993-1-8 Table 4.1. Accounts for the ratio of filler metal strength to base metal strength.

formulas.label: Fillet Weld Design Resistance per Unit Length (EN 1993-1-8)
formulas.description: Design shear resistance of fillet weld per unit length per EN 1993-1-8 Eq. 4.1. Uses directional or simplified method.

formulas.label: Fillet Weld Design Strength (AWS D1.1 / AISC)
formulas.description: AWS D1.1 Clause 2.4.2.3 / AISC 360-22 Eq. J2-3: Fillet weld nominal strength = 0.60 × Fu_E × A_we × (1.0 + 0.5 sin^1.5(theta)). For SMAW, phi=0.75 (LRFD).

formulas.label: Weld Design Demand — Axial Tension Stress
formulas.description: Axial tensile stress in weld group from applied N_Ed

formulas.label: Weld Design Demand — Direct Shear Stress
formulas.description: Direct shear stress in weld group from applied V_Ed

formulas.label: Weld Group Polar Moment of Inertia
formulas.description: Polar moment of inertia of weld group about its centroid (elastic method). Simplified for rectangular weld groups.

formulas.label: Weld Design Demand — Torsional Shear Stress
formulas.description: Maximum shear stress in weld group due to torsional moment T_Ed. Uses elastic torsion formula: tau = (T * r) / J.

formulas.label: Weld Design Demand — Bending Stress
formulas.description: Maximum bending stress in weld group from moment M_Ed and out-of-plane eccentricity
formulas.expression: fw_Ed_moment = (M_Ed > 0 || weld_eccentricity_z_mm > 0) ? ((M_Ed + V_Ed * weld_eccentricity_z_mm / 1000) * 1e6 * weld_length_mm / 2) / Max((number_of_weld_segments * Power(weld_length_mm, 3) / 12), 1) : 0

formulas.label: Weld Resultant Design Stress (Vector Sum)
formulas.description: Resultant weld stress combining all directional components. For elastic method: sqrt(sigma_perp^2 + tau_perp^2 + tau_par^2).

formulas.label: Total Weld Resistance (EN 1993-1-8 Directional Method)
formulas.description: Design resistance of fillet weld expressed as stress limit. Per EN 1993-1-8 Eq. 4.1: fw_Rd = fu / (beta_w × gamma_M2 × sqrt(3)). The directional method checks three stress components against limits.

formulas.label: Weld Utilization Ratio (Demand / Capacity)
formulas.description: Demand-capacity ratio for the weld. UC <= 1.0 indicates adequate weld strength per code.

formulas.label: Base Metal Gross Area (Connected Part)
formulas.description: Gross cross-sectional area of the connected base material

formulas.label: Base Metal Net Area (Account for bolt holes if present)
formulas.description: Net area of the connected base material. Deducts bolt hole area if applicable (assumes no holes for all-welded connections).

formulas.label: Base Metal Yielding Resistance (N_Rd_gross)
formulas.description: Design resistance of base metal gross section yielding per EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.3(2) / AISC 360-22 Eq. J4-1: N_Rd = A_g × fy / gamma_M0

formulas.label: Base Metal Rupture Resistance (N_Rd_net)
formulas.description: Design resistance of base metal net section rupture per EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.3(4) / AISC 360-22 Eq. J4-2: N_Rd = A_n × fu / gamma_M2

formulas.label: Base Metal Yielding Utilization
formulas.description: Demand-capacity ratio for base metal gross section yielding

formulas.label: Base Metal Rupture Utilization
formulas.description: Demand-capacity ratio for base metal net section rupture

formulas.label: Base Metal Shear Resistance (Block Shear)
formulas.description: Base metal shear resistance per EN 1993-1-8 §3.10.2 / AISC 360-22 Eq. J4-4: V_Rd = (0.6 × fu × A_nv) / gamma_M2

formulas.label: Base Metal Shear Utilization
formulas.description: Demand-capacity ratio for base metal shear (block shear) resistance

formulas.label: Base Metal Combined Shear + Tension Interaction
formulas.description: Combined shear and tension interaction check. Per EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.1(5) / AISC 360-22 Eq. J4-3b: (N_Ed/N_Rd) + (V_Ed/V_Rd) <= 1.0 (simplified linear interaction).
formulas.expression: UC_combined_base_metal = (N_Ed > 0 && V_Ed > 0) ? Min(Abs(N_Ed) / N_Rd_yield + Abs(V_Ed) / V_Rd_bm_shear, Max(Abs(N_Ed) / N_Rd_yield, Abs(V_Ed) / V_Rd_bm_shear)) : Max(Abs(N_Ed) / N_Rd_yield, Abs(V_Ed) / V_Rd_bm_shear)

formulas.label: Governing Utilization Ratio (Overall Connection)
formulas.description: Highest utilization ratio across all weld and base metal checks. Determines the governing failure mode.
formulas.expression: UC_governing = Max(UC_weld, UC_base_metal_yield, UC_base_metal_rupture, UC_base_metal_shear, UC_combined_base_metal)

formulas.label: Governing Failure Mode Description
formulas.description: Identifies the governing failure mode based on the highest utilization ratio among all checks performed
formulas.expression: governing_failure_mode = (UC_governing == UC_weld) ? "Weld metal shear failure (fillet weld resistance governs)" : (UC_governing == UC_base_metal_yield) ? "Base metal gross section yielding governs" : (UC_governing == UC_base_metal_rupture) ? "Base metal net section rupture governs" : (UC_governing == UC_base_metal_shear) ? "Base metal block shear / edge tearing governs" : "Combined base metal shear + tension interaction governs"

formulas.label: Fatigue Stress Check (EN 1993-1-9 / AWS D1.1)
formulas.description: Fatigue assessment per EN 1993-1-9 Eq. 6.1: gamma_Ff × Delta_sigma_E2 <= Delta_sigma_C / gamma_Mf. Simplified: Delta_sigma <= FAT_class / gamma_Ff.

formulas.label: Lamellar Tearing Risk Assessment
formulas.description: Lamellar tearing susceptibility check for T-joints and cruciform joints with through-thickness tension. Per EN 1011-2 and EN 1993-1-10 guidance.

formulas.label: Hydrogen-Induced Cracking (HIC) Risk Assessment
formulas.description: Risk assessment for hydrogen-induced cold cracking. Based on CEV, thickness, hydrogen level, and preheat as per EN 1011-2 Annex C / IIW recommendations.

formulas.label: Estimated Weld Metal Volume
formulas.description: Estimated weld metal volume per unit length for cost and consumable estimation. Fillet: A_weld = 0.5 × s^2 (single fillet). Groove: variable by bevel prep.
formulas.expression: A_net_mm2 = (weld_type == "Fillet Weld (double-sided)" || weld_type == "Fillet Weld (single-sided)") ? (fillet_weld_leg_size_mm - 0.5 * fillet_weld_leg_size_mm) * weld_length_mm : groove_weld_depth_mm * weld_length_mm

formulas.label: Estimated Angular Distortion (Transverse Shrinkage)
formulas.description: Estimated angular distortion per unit length due to weld contraction. Based on heat input and plate thickness. Approximate per Okerblom / AWS Welding Handbook.

formulas.label: Net Area in Shear (Block Shear Path)
formulas.description: Net area along the shear path for block shear rupture check. Accounts for the welded connection geometry (no bolt holes for all-welded joints).

formulas.label: Block Shear Rupture Resistance (AISC J4.3 / EN 1993-1-8)
formulas.description: Block shear rupture resistance per AISC 360-22 J4.3 / EN 1993-1-8 §3.10.2. The lesser of: (1) 0.6 × Fu × A_nv + U_bs × Fu × A_nt, or (2) 0.6 × Fy × A_gv + U_bs × Fu × A_nt.
formulas.expression: block_shear_resistance_kN = Min(0.6 * fu_bm * A_nv + 1.0 * fu_bm * (A_n_bm / 2), 0.6 * fy_bm * A_g_bm + 1.0 * fu_bm * (A_n_bm / 2)) / partial_factor_gamma_M2 / 1000

formulas.label: Block Shear Utilization
formulas.description: Demand-capacity ratio for block shear rupture

formulas.label: Weld Joint Efficiency (for groove welds)
formulas.description: Joint efficiency for groove welds. CJP = 100%. PJP = effective throat / base thickness ratio (limited by weld depth).

formulas.label: Minimum Fillet Weld Size Check (EN 1993-1-8 / AWS D1.1)
formulas.description: Minimum fillet weld size per AWS D1.1 Table 5.4 (based on thicker part thickness) and EN 1993-1-8 §4.5.1(1). For t <= 6mm: min s = 3mm. For t = 6–12mm: min s = 5mm. For t = 12–20mm: min s = 6mm. For t = 20–38mm: min s = 8mm. For t = 38–57mm: min s = 10mm. For t = 57–150mm: min s = 12mm.

formulas.label: Maximum Fillet Weld Size Check (Edge Distance)
formulas.description: Maximum fillet weld size along edges. AWS D1.1 Cl. 2.3.2.3: For material thickness < 6mm, max s = t (edge). For t >= 6mm, max s = t - 2mm.
formulas.expression: max_weld_size_check = (weld_type == "Fillet Weld (double-sided)" || weld_type == "Fillet Weld (single-sided)") ? ((base_material_thickness_connected_mm < 6) ? ((fillet_weld_leg_size_mm <= base_material_thickness_connected_mm) ? 1 : 0) : ((fillet_weld_leg_size_mm <= base_material_thickness_connected_mm - 2) ? 1 : 0)) : 0

formulas.label: Minimum Effective Weld Length Check
formulas.description: Minimum effective weld length per EN 1993-1-8 §4.5.1(2): L_eff,min = max(6 × a, 40mm). Also AWS D1.1: minimum effective length = 4 × weld size (but not less than 38mm).
formulas.expression: min_weld_length_check = (weld_type == "Fillet Weld (double-sided)" || weld_type == "Fillet Weld (single-sided)") ? ((weld_length_mm >= Max(6 * a_eff, 40)) ? 1 : 0) : 0

outputs.label: Governing Utilization Ratio
outputs.description: Overall governing demand-capacity ratio for the welded connection

outputs.label: Governing Failure Mode
outputs.description: Identifies the controlling failure mode based on highest utilization

outputs.label: Weld Utilization (Weld Metal)
outputs.description: Demand-capacity ratio for weld metal shear strength

outputs.label: Base Metal Yielding Utilization
outputs.description: Demand-capacity ratio for base metal gross section yielding

outputs.label: Base Metal Rupture Utilization
outputs.description: Demand-capacity ratio for base metal net section rupture

outputs.label: Base Metal Shear Utilization
outputs.description: Demand-capacity ratio for base metal shear (block shear)

outputs.label: Base Metal Combined Shear+Tension Utilization
outputs.description: Demand-capacity ratio for base metal combined shear and tension interaction

outputs.label: Block Shear Utilization
outputs.description: Demand-capacity ratio for block shear rupture

outputs.label: Maximum Weld Resultant Stress
outputs.description: Resultant weld stress at critical point (vector sum of all directional components)

outputs.label: Weld Design Resistance (Stress Limit)
outputs.description: Design resistance of the weld metal expressed as stress limit per EN 1993-1-8 directional method

outputs.label: Total Weld Effective Area
outputs.description: Total effective throat area of the weld group

outputs.label: AWS D1.1/AISC Weld Design Strength
outputs.description: Nominal weld strength per AWS D1.1/AISC 360 (LRFD with phi=0.75)

outputs.label: Fatigue Stress Check Result
outputs.description: PASS/FAIL assessment of fatigue stress range against detail category

outputs.label: Hydrogen Cracking Risk Assessment
outputs.description: Risk level for hydrogen-induced cold cracking based on CEV, thickness, preheat, and hydrogen class

outputs.label: Lamellar Tearing Risk Assessment
outputs.description: Risk assessment for lamellar tearing in thick T-joints and cruciform connections with through-thickness tension

outputs.label: Minimum Weld Size Check
outputs.description: Verification that selected fillet weld size meets code minimum requirements

outputs.label: Maximum Weld Size Check
outputs.description: Verification that selected fillet weld size does not exceed code maximum at edges

outputs.label: Minimum Weld Length Check
outputs.description: Verification that effective weld length meets minimum code requirements

outputs.label: Weld Joint Efficiency
outputs.description: Weld joint efficiency ratio (groove welds). CJP = 100%, PJP typically 50–80%.

outputs.label: Estimated Weld Metal Volume
outputs.description: Estimated volume of deposited weld metal for cost estimation

outputs.label: Estimated Angular Distortion
outputs.description: Estimated angular distortion from weld contraction

outputs.label: Base Material Gross Section Area
outputs.description: Gross cross-sectional area of the connected base material

outputs.label: Weld Resistance per Unit Length (EN 1993-1-8)
outputs.description: Design resistance of fillet weld per unit length using EN 1993-1-8 directional method

rules.message: Minimum fillet weld leg size is 3 mm per EN 1993-1-8 §4.5.1(1) and AWS D1.1 Table 5.4.
Effective weld length must be at least 40 mm (EN 1993-1-8 §4.5.1(2)). Shorter welds cannot develop full strength.
Axial tension cannot be negative. Use positive values for tension; use zero if no axial load.
Shear force cannot be negative. Use positive values for shear; use zero if no shear load.
Base material thickness must be at least 3 mm for welding. Thinner materials require specialized procedures.

Filler metal tensile strength is less than 80% of base metal tensile strength. Undermatching filler metal may reduce connection capacity. Consider overmatching or strength-matching filler metal per AWS D1.1 Clause 3.3.
CEV > 0.45 and thickness > 30 mm: preheat is mandatory per EN 1011-2. Verify that preheat temperature meets minimum recommendations.
Low-temperature service (T < -20°C) with thick material (t > 25 mm): brittle fracture assessment per EN 1993-1-10 is required. Verify Charpy toughness requirements are met.
CEV > 0.50 requires minimum 100°C preheat per EN 1011-2 Table C.1, regardless of thickness. Insufficient preheat increases hydrogen cracking risk.

Fillet weld leg size exceeds the thickness of the connected part. This may indicate an uneconomical design. Consider increasing base material thickness or using partial-joint-penetration groove weld instead.
High heat input (> 3.5 kJ/mm) on thin material (< 10 mm) may cause excessive distortion, burn-through, and HAZ degradation. Reduce heat input or use pulsed welding parameters.

rules.condition: load_type_cyclic <> "Static / Quasi-static" AND fatigue_stress_range_mpa <= 0 AND fatigue_detail_category <> "Not applicable (static only)"
rules.message: Cyclic load type selected but fatigue stress range is zero or missing. Enter the expected stress range for fatigue assessment.

smart_warnings.message: CONNECTION OVERSTRESSED: Governing utilization ratio UC = [UC_governing] exceeds 1.0. The connection is insufficient for the applied loads. Actions: (1) Increase weld leg size, (2) Use higher-strength filler metal, (3) Increase weld length, (4) Add stiffeners/backing bars, (5) Change connection geometry to reduce eccentricity. Verify base material strength is also adequate.

CONNECTION HIGHLY STRESSED: Governing utilization UC = [UC_governing] is between 0.85 and 1.0. Consider increasing weld size or optimizing geometry for economical design. Verify that all quality requirements (NDT, WPS qualification) are strictly met for this utilization level.

Weld metal governs over base metal. This is acceptable if filler metal strength matches or overmatches base metal. If weld metal is undermatched, base metal strength may not be fully utilized. Consider checking electrode classification vs. base metal grade compatibility (AWS D1.1 Cl. 3.3.2).

Thick section (t > 50 mm) in low-temperature service (T < -15°C): fracture critical. EN 1993-1-10 requires specific Charpy toughness verification. Recommended CVN at T_min: ≥ 47 J (based on K_mat requirement). Verify WPS includes low-hydrogen process and controlled heat input.

smart_warnings.condition: seismic_application CONTAINS "High ductility" OR seismic_application CONTAINS "SFRS"
smart_warnings.message: Seismic weld demand is critical per AISC 341 / EN 1998-1. Weld must satisfy notch-toughness requirements (CVN ≥ 27 J at T_min for SMF per AISC 341-22 §A3.1c). Filler metal must have minimum Charpy requirements. Pre-qualified WPS per AWS D1.1 Cl. 7 or AWS D1.8 required. Consider using demand-critical weld category with 100% UT/MT inspection.

FATIGUE FAILURE: The weld detail cannot sustain the applied stress range for the required number of cycles. Options: (1) Increase throat size to reduce stress, (2) Change detail category to higher FAT class (e.g., improve weld toe profile, grinding, TIG dressing), (3) Use prequalified CJP groove weld with smooth transition instead of fillet weld, (4) Reduce stress range by increasing member stiffness.

smart_warnings.condition: base_material_grade_connected CONTAINS "S460" OR base_material_grade_connected CONTAINS "S500" OR base_material_grade_connected CONTAINS "S700" OR base_material_grade_connected CONTAINS "A514"
smart_warnings.message: High-strength steel selected (Fy > 420 MPa). Verify WPS is qualified specifically for this steel grade. High-strength steels are more susceptible to HAZ softening, hydrogen cracking, and require stricter heat input control. Pre-qualified WPS per AWS D1.1 may not cover all high-strength grades — procedure qualification records (PQR) required per AWS D1.1 Cl. 4 and EN ISO 15614-1.

High execution class (EXC3/EXC4) selected. EN 1090-2 requires: (1) Welding coordinator qualification per EN ISO 14731, (2) Welder qualification per EN ISO 9606-1, (3) WPS per EN ISO 15609-1 with PQR per EN ISO 15614-1, (4) NDT per specified level (VT + RT/UT + MT/PT), (5) Traceability system for consumables and welders. Verify all documentation is in order.

combined_uncertainty.notes: Combined standard uncertainty estimated at 8.5% based on GUM linear approximation. Dominant contributors: fillet weld leg size (±1mm, ~5.5% sensitivity), electrode tensile strength (±30 MPa, ~4.2% sensitivity). A combined UC of 1.08 or less under best-estimate inputs could result in actual UC exceeding 1.0 at 95% confidence. Consider applying a design margin of 5% for critical connections.

fmea.failureMode: Fillet weld shear failure — weld metal rupture
fmea.effect: Brittle or ductile fracture through the weld throat. Loss of load path. Possible collapse of connection.
fmea.control_measure: EN 1993-1-8 §4.3.2 directional method + AWS D1.1 §2.4 strength verification. WPS qualification per EN ISO 15614-1. Verify filler metal strength classification. NDT: UT of critical welds per EN ISO 17640.
fmea.description: Governs when UC_weld > UC_base_metal limits. Ensure UC_weld ≤ 1.0 with appropriate partial factors.

fmea.failureMode: Base metal yielding / net section rupture at connection
fmea.effect: Gross yielding or net section tearing of connected member adjacent to weld. Possible progressive collapse if tension member.
fmea.control_measure: EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.3 yielding/rupture checks + AISC 360-22 J4.1. Verify net area is adequate for applied tension. Consider load path eccentricity.
fmea.description: Check UC_base_metal_yield and UC_base_metal_rupture. Net section rupture is particularly critical for connections with bolt holes.

fmea.failureMode: Block shear rupture at welded connection
fmea.effect: Tearing failure along shear path + tension path. Sudden loss of connection capacity.
fmea.control_measure: AISC 360-22 J4.3 block shear check. EN 1993-1-8 §3.10.2. Ensure shear and tension planes have adequate net area.
fmea.description: Block shear considered in UC_block_shear. Critical for short, heavily loaded welded connections.

fmea.failureMode: Fatigue crack initiation and propagation at weld toe
fmea.effect: Crack initiation at stress concentration (weld toe/root). Propagation through base metal under cyclic loading. Progressive failure.
fmea.control_measure: EN 1993-1-9 fatigue assessment + AWS D1.1 Clause 9. Detail category selection per joint type. NDT: MT or PT at 10× design life intervals for fatigue-critical welds.
fmea.description: Fatigue verification in FAT_CHK. If Delta_sigma > FAT_class / gamma_Mf, detail must be improved.

fmea.failureMode: Hydrogen-induced cold cracking (HIC) in HAZ or weld metal
fmea.effect: Delayed cracking (hours to days after welding). Complete weld failure under service load. Difficult to detect before loading.
fmea.control_measure: EN 1011-2 preheat + hydrogen control. Select low-hydrogen consumables (Class A or B per ISO 3690). Control interpass temperature. Post-weld hydrogen release heat treatment (250–300°C × 2+ h) for CEV > 0.45 and t > 30 mm.
fmea.description: HIC risk assessed in hydrogen_cracking_risk output. HIGH RISK requires immediate corrective action.

fmea.failureMode: Lamellar tearing in thick T-joints or cruciform connections
fmea.effect: Stepwise cracking in HAZ/base metal under through-thickness tension. Material separation parallel to rolled surface.
fmea.control_measure: Use Z-grade steel (EN 10164) for through-thickness tension zones. Buttering technique with low-strength filler. Reduce weld volume and restraint. Redesign joint to avoid through-thickness tension.
fmea.description: Lamellar tearing assessed in lamellar_tearing_risk output. HIGH RISK requires joint redesign or Z-grade steel.

fmea.failureMode: Brittle fracture at low temperature (insufficient notch toughness)
fmea.effect: Sudden, catastrophic fracture without plastic deformation. Initiation at weld defect or stress concentration.
fmea.control_measure: EN 1993-1-10 fracture toughness verification. Select steel with adequate Charpy energy at T_min (typically 27 J or 47 J). Verify filler metal CVN per AWS A5 series / EN ISO 2560. Use NDT (UT) to detect critical flaws.
fmea.description: Brittle fracture risk is HIGH when T_min < -20°C and t > 25 mm. Verify CVN requirements.

fmea.failureMode: Excessive distortion and residual stresses from welding
fmea.effect: Out-of-tolerance geometry, misalignment, locked-in stresses. Serviceability failure and reduced buckling capacity.
fmea.control_measure: EN 1090-2 tolerance requirements. Use balanced welding sequence, backstep technique, and adequate restraint. Consider pre-setting or post-weld straightening. Control heat input (HI < 2.0 kJ/mm preferred for thin sections).
fmea.description: Distortion estimated in distortion_estimate_mm. If > 1.5 mm, welding sequence and clamping need review.

required_entries: Design code and edition (EN 1993-1-8:2005 + A2:2011 / AWS D1.1/D1.1M:2020) and applicable National Annex
Base material grade, thickness, and material certification reference (EN 10204 3.1 certificate)
Filler metal classification, lot number, and manufacturer certificate of compliance
Welding process, WPS reference number, and PQR reference (EN ISO 15614-1 or AWS D1.1 Cl. 4)
Welder qualification records (EN ISO 9606-1 or AWS D1.1 Cl. 5) for all welders assigned to the joint
Preheat and interpass temperature records (temperature-indicating crayons or thermocouple readings)
Heat input record for each pass (V, I, travel speed per WPS range)
NDT/Inspection reports: VT, UT, RT, MT, PT as applicable per EN ISO 17637, 17640, 17636, etc.
Fatigue design check calculations and detail category selection (EN 1993-1-9 Table 8.1–8.10)
Seismic design verification if applicable (AISC 341 / EN 1998-1)
Coating / corrosion protection system per ISO 12944
EN 1090-2 execution class declaration and fabrication inspection records
Deviations, non-conformances, and repair procedures (including re-welding NDT)

helpers_required.name: lookup_material_properties
helpers_required.description: Map steel grade to fy, fu, CEV, and Charpy requirements per EN 10025 and ASTM standards

helpers_required.name: lookup_electrode_strength
helpers_required.description: Map electrode classification to nominal tensile strength and hydrogen class per AWS A5 / EN ISO 2560

helpers_required.name: lookup_fatigue_detail_category
helpers_required.description: Retrieve FAT class and Detail category parameters from the selected fatigue detail category for EN 1993-1-9 verification

helpers_required.name: calculate_weld_group_properties
helpers_required.description: Compute weld group centroid, polar moment of inertia, and section modulus for arbitrary weld group configurations

helpers_required.name: assess_brittle_fracture_risk
helpers_required.description: Assess brittle fracture risk per EN 1993-1-10 based on material grade, thickness, min service temperature, and Charpy energy

helpers_required.name: calculate_preheat_temperature
helpers_required.description: Calculate minimum preheat temperature per EN 1011-2 / IIW formula based on CEV, thickness, hydrogen level, and heat input

primary.title: Eurocode 3: Design of steel structures — Part 1-8: Design of joints
primary.description: Governing European standard for welded joint design including fillet weld directional method, effective throat/width, and detailing rules.

primary.title: Structural Welding Code — Steel
primary.description: Governing US standard for structural steel welding. Covers prequalified joints, filler metal requirements, workmanship, and inspection.

primary.title: Specification for Structural Steel Buildings
primary.description: AISC specification for welded connection design including weld strength, base metal checks, and block shear.

secondary.title: Eurocode 3: Fatigue strength of steel structures
secondary.description: Fatigue assessment of steel structures including weld detail categories.

secondary.title: Eurocode 3: Material toughness and through-thickness properties
secondary.description: Brittle fracture and lamellar tearing assessment based on thickness, steel grade, and service temperature.

secondary.title: Welding — Recommendations for welding of metallic materials — Part 2: Arc welding of ferritic steels
secondary.description: Preheat temperature recommendations based on CEV, thickness, and hydrogen level. HIC prevention guidelines.

secondary.title: Execution of steel structures and aluminium structures — Part 2: Technical requirements for steel structures
secondary.description: Execution classes (EXC1-EXC4), fabrication tolerances, and inspection requirements for structural steel.

secondary.title: Welding — Fusion-welded joints in steel, nickel, titanium and their alloys — Quality levels for imperfections
secondary.description: Quality levels (B, C, D) for weld imperfections across all joint types and welding processes.

reference_code.standards_organization: CEN (European Committee for Standardization) + AWS (American Welding Society) + AISC (American Institute of Steel Construction)
